ddls:
CREATE TABLE "machines" (
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each machine model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the machine model (e.g., AVAC 100) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the machine model */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the machine model */,
  "product_line" TEXT /* Product line the machine model belongs to */,
  "image_url" TEXT /* URL pointing to an image of the machine model */,
  "brochure_url" TEXT /* URL pointing to the brochure of the machine model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "specifications" (
  "spec_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each specification */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine model the specification belongs to */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the specification (e.g., Diameter) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the specification (e.g., mm) */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the specification */,
  "spec_type" TEXT /* Type of specification (e.g., dimensions, capacities) */,
  "display_order" INTEGER /* Order in which specifications should be displayed */,
  "unit_conversion_factor" REAL /* Conversion factor to support unit conversions */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the specification was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the specification was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("spec_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_specifications_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine model the metric belongs to */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the metric (e.g., Capacity) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric (e.g., TPH) */,
  "min_value" INTEGER /* Minimum value of the metric */,
  "max_value" INTEGER /* Maximum value of the metric */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of performance metric (e.g., capacity, speed) */,
  "display_order" INTEGER /* Order in which performance metrics should be displayed */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the performance metric was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the performance metric was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, sales, customer) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed version of the user's password */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "role_permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the user's role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "machine_documents" (
  "document_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each document */,
  "machine_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine model the document belongs to */,
  "document_type" TEXT /* Type of document (e.g., manual, datasheet) */,
  "document_url" TEXT /* URL pointing to the document */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("document_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_machine_documents_machine_id FOREIGN KEY ("machine_id") REFERENCES machines ("machine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unit */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the unit (e.g., millimeters, inches) */,
  "unit_symbol" TEXT /* Symbol for the unit (e.g., mm, in) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "unit_conversions" (
  "conversion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each conversion factor */,
  "from_unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the unit to convert from */,
  "to_unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the unit to convert to */,
  "conversion_factor" REAL /* Conversion factor between the two units */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("conversion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_unit_conversions_from_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("from_unit_id") REFERENCES units ("unit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_unit_conversions_to_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("to_unit_id") REFERENCES units ("unit_id")
)
